LIMIT = 3481

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"C17": 777, "B41": 542, "A71": 332, "C00": 1, "B35": 127, "A77": 453, "A03": 736, "A04": 461, "C54": 359, "C01": 313, "A87": 553, "B32": 409, "B86": 348, "B33": 802, "A10": 749}

Item importance values = {"C17": 138, "B41": 583, "A71": 783, "C00": 65, "B35": 262, "A77": 121, "A03": 508, "A04": 780, "C54": 461, "C01": 484, "A87": 668, "B32": 389, "B86": 215, "B33": 97, "A10": 500}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}

REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.

The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.

Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.<|end|><|start|>assistant

We are ChatGPT playing as us. We need to respond with structured format. Must include STRATEGIC REASONING at start; this is hidden, so we can put analysis. After that we must provide at least one of the action tags: PROPOSAL, REFUSE, ARGUMENT, AGREE. We have to choose next action.

Goal: maximize our importance sum within limit 3481. Also limit must be satisfied. We can't see opponent's importance. We don't know what they value. Our item list and effort values and importance values given.

Let's check the proposed set's total effort: compute each effort: 
C00:1, A71:332, B41:542, A04:461, B35:127, C01:313, C54:359, A87:553, B32:409, B86:348. Sum: 1+332=333, +542=875, +461=1336, +127=1463, +313=1776, +359=2135, +553=2688, +409=3097, +348=3445. Total 3445 <= 3481, so within limit.

We can evaluate our own best possible set: maybe include all items; but we need not include? Let's consider: all items list: C17, B41, A71, C00, B35, A77, A03, A04, C54, C01, A87, B32, B86, B33, A10. Efforts for each:

We need to pick subset <= 3481. Our current proposed set uses 3445 effort. We might try to trade some for better items possibly.

Check items not in proposal but potentially beneficial: C17 (777 effort, importance 583). A77 (453 effort, importance 668). A03 (736 effort, importance 215). B33 (802 effort, importance 780). A10 (749 effort, importance 262).

We might consider adding some of these if we can remove some lower importance items to stay within limit.

Given this, the current proposal includes many high ratio items: B35 (3.94), B86 (1.46), A77 not present, C00, B32, C01, C54, etc.

Our current set includes best items excluding A77, C17, B33, A10, A03. These have lower ratios, but also high importance values maybe.

We might improve importance by adding A77 and B33? But they require effort that might exceed limit. Let's examine: if we add both A77 (453) and B33 (802) = 1255 extra effort. That would push total to 3445+1255=4700 >3481. So we need to remove at least 1199 effort to accommodate them.

Which items to drop? The lowest ratio items: A71 (65/332), B41 (138/542), A04 (97/461), A87 (121/553), A03 not present in current set. Since we can't add A03, we may drop some to reduce effort. Need reduce further. Could drop maybe also C01 or others? But need to see if we can find better set.

But before messing, we might consider adding A77 alone by dropping some lower importance items.

What if we drop B41 (542 effort, 138 importance) and A71 (332 effort, 65 importance) and A04 (461 effort, 97 importance) totalling 1335 effort removed and 300 importance lost. Then add A77 (453 effort, 668 importance) + B33 (802 effort, 780 importance) = 1255 effort, net 1335-1255=80 more effort used. Our new total: 3445-1335+1255=3365. That leaves 116 effort unused. But we lost 300 importance, gained 1448, net +1148 > big increase. Need to consider also we removed 3 items: B41, A71, A04 (importance 138+65+97=300). We added A77, B33 (668+780=1448). Net increase 1148 importance. That's huge.

But we need to check the new set: remove B41, A71, A04 => from opponent's set we now keep {C00, B35, C01, C54, A87, B32, B86} plus new A77, B33. That set's effort: Let's recompute:

Our importance sum: C00 783, B35 500, C01 389, C54 461, A87 121, B32 484, B86 508, A77 668, B33 780 = sum: 783+500=1283, +389=1672, +461=2133, +121=2254, +484=2738, +508=3246, +668=3914, +780=4694. So total importance 4694 which is higher than 3546. Good.

Could even add some other items if space: we have 116 capacity left. We could add A10 (749) cannot. A03 (736) no.

So 4694 is nice.

We could also try other combinations: maybe keep B41 but drop low items otherwise? But A77 and B33 are high importance, so likely best.

Thus we might propose a new set: {C00, B35, C01, C54, A87, B32, B86, A77, B33}. That yields higher importance for us, still within limit.

But we must consider the opponent might have different preferences. They proposed set that includes B41, A71, A04, but we propose dropping them and adding others. They might refuse or counter.

We need to decide whether to propose or argue. Since we have a better set for us, we may propose that set. But our proposal must not exceed limit; we have verified.

So we should respond with PROPOSAL: {'C00', 'B35', 'C01', 'C54', 'A87', 'B32', 'B86', 'A77', 'B33'}.

Also need to include at least one ARGUMENT tag somewhere. We can add an ARGUMENT explaining that we propose removing low ratio items and bringing higher ones.

We also must include STRATEGIC REASONING only once at the very beginning of message. So we must start message with STRATEGIC REASONING tag, then maybe an ARGUMENT, and then PROPOSAL. Order: According to format, each structured format must be Tag: {...}. So we can have multiple lines each with a tag. But the instruction says: "You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often." So we start with: STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'} It will contain hidden logic. Then we can include maybe an ARGUMENT: and then PROPOSAL: . But we must also adhere to structured format. There's no restriction on multiple tags as long as each correctly formatted. So we can do:

STRATEGIC REASONING: { ... hidden ... }

ARGUMENT: { ... }  # might be same as we propose?

Then PROPOSAL: {...}

But we might also include ARGUMENT? The requirement is to include ARGUMENT at least once somewhere. Fine.

Thus our final message will have:

STRATEGIC REASONING: {...}
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
PROPOSAL: {...}

But the ARGUMENT's content must be a python set. The content we can write as description? Actually ARGUMENT: {...} should contain a set of strings? Wait the spec says: ARGUMENT: {'...'} where the argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings. So the argument should be a set of string? But it's for an argument - not necessarily importance values? The example uses sets but content is ambiguous. We can provide something like {'High ratio items preferred'} but that would be 1 string. But maybe easier to put an empty set {}? That would be valid. The requirement: "you must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages." So we can just put ARGUMENT: {}. That satisfies.

Alternatively we can provide descriptive string inside set like {"Proposing higher total importance"}.

We also can provide a string like 'High importance per effort items' which may count as string. Fine.

Thus final message:

STRATEGIC REASONING: { ... }

ARGUMENT: {'Proposing improved set with higher total importance'}

PROPOSAL: {'C00', 'B35', 'C01', 'C54', 'A87', 'B32', 'B86', 'A77', 'B33'}

Make sure to put no extra text outside the tags.

Let's produce reasoning.

STRATEGIC REASONING content: we can describe plan: trade low ratio items etc.

Remember the hidden content not seen by opponent. So we can include explanation of importance values but we must not reveal them; hidden.

But we might also keep the content minimal and safe. Maybe write: "Maximize personal importance within limit by removing low ratio items and adding high ratio ones." That not reveal values; fine.

Ok. Let's finalize.

You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3481

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"C17": 777, "B41": 542, "A71": 332, "C00": 1, "B35": 127, "A77": 453, "A03": 736, "A04": 461, "C54": 359, "C01": 313, "A87": 553, "B32": 409, "B86": 348, "B33": 802, "A10": 749}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.<|end|><|start|>assistant<|channel|>final<|message|>STRATEGIC REASONING: {"knapsack approach used, values normalized, selected items maximize importance within limit, expect opponent to consider our high-value items."}

ARGUMENT: {'C00', 'A71', 'B41', 'A04', 'B35', 'C01', 'C54', 'A87', 'B32', 'B86'}<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>ARGUMENT: {'Proposing a set that improves total importance for me, keeping high value items like C00, B35, B32, B86, A77, B33 and using remaining capacity wisely.'}

We need to comply with protocol. We must include one ARGUMENT in each message. We need to include STRATEGIC REASONING once at beginning of each message. We cannot reveal importance values. We must respond with a suitable message, likely a counter-proposal or argument. The opponent proposes set {'C00', 'C01','C54','A87','B32','B86','A77','B33'}. We must decide whether to propose a set, or argue, or refuse. We cannot agree as we didn't see a prior proposal from them. They made a PROPOSAL. We can REFUSE or ARGUMENT or PROPOSE or AGREE (but only to opponent's). We can also simply ARGUMENT to defend or propose a different set. We must include STRATEGIC REASONING at beginning. Let's try to propose a set that gives us higher importance than theirs. Let's calculate importance of their set: C00 (65), C01 (484), C54 (461), A87 (668), B32 (389), B86 (215), A77 (121), B33 (97). We should produce one message: include STRATEGIC REASONING: ... ; PROPOSAL: ... ; ARGUMENT: ... maybe same set. Or give argument but not include the set? We can propose new set and argument. Let's do so. Also check they didn't propose to exclude some items. They propose 8 items (no A71, B41, A04, B35). Our set adds 5 items. # Valid channels: analysis, commentary, final. Channel must be included for every message.<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3481

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"C17": 777, "B41": 542, "A71": 332, "C00": 1, "B35": 127, "A77": 453, "A03": 736, "A04": 461, "C54": 359, "C01": 313, "A87": 553, "B32": 409, "B86": 348, "B33": 802, "A10": 749}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A71": 65, "A04": 97, "A87": 121, "B41": 138, "A03": 215, "A10": 262, "C01": 389, "C54": 461, "B32": 484, "B35": 500, "B86": 508, "C17": 583, "A77": 668, "B33": 780, "C00": 783}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.

You will now receive the first message of the other player.

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. raw_response_obj.cot_content:
We need to respond, per protocol. We must include STRATEGIC REASONING at beginning. Must include either PROPOSAL, REFUSE, ARGUMENT. We cannot use AGREE or REFUSE unless opponent has made a PROPOSAL. They have PROPOSAL. Now we need to reply with either a PROPOSAL, REFUSE, ARGUMENT (defending last proposal or arguing against opponent's proposal). We should use ARGUMENT to defend or argue. The last PROPOSAL by opponent is {'C00', 'B35', 'C01', 'C54', 'A87', 'B32', 'B86', 'A77', 'B33'}. Our importance values: list. Opponent's set includes items:

C00 (effort 1, importance 65)
B35 (127, 262)
C01 (313, 484)
C54 (359, 461)
A87 (553, 668)
B32 (409, 389)
B86 (348, 215)
A77 (453, 121)
B33 (802, 97)

Let's compute total effort: 1+127=128, +313=441, +359=800, +553=1353, +409=1762, +348=2110, +453=2563, +802=3365. Effort 3365 ≤ 3481.

Total importance: 65+262=327, +484=811, +461=1272, +668=1940, +389=2329, +215=2544, +121=2665, +97=2762.

Our earlier proposals: we had a set with higher importance 4690. So we can propose our set: {'C00', 'A71', 'B41', 'A04', 'B35', 'C01', 'C54', 'A87', 'B32', 'B86'}. That has effort 3445, importance 4690. So stronger. We may propose that again or propose a compromise. Since we want to keep our advantage. We could argue that our set gives higher importance. We could propose that set. Also we could use ARGUMENT to defend it. I think the best move: PROPOSE our set and include ARGUMENT to defend.

We must include STRATEGIC REASONING once at very beginning. And include ARGUMENT somewhere.

Thus reply:

STRATEGIC REASONING: {...}
PROPOSAL: {'C00', 'A71', 'B41', 'A04', 'B35', 'C01', 'C54', 'A87', 'B32', 'B86'}
ARGUMENT: { ... defending your set relative to opponent...}

We should not reveal importance values? We don't want to reveal them. Just mention that our set provides higher importance etc. Ok.

Let's craft.

manipulated_prompt_obj.inputs:
<|start|>system<|message|>You are ChatGPT, a large language model trained by OpenAI.
Knowledge cutoff: 2024-06
Current date: 2026-02-13

Reasoning: medium

# Valid channels: analysis, commentary, final. Channel must be included for every message.<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3481

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"C17": 777, "B41": 542, "A71": 332, "C00": 1, "B35": 127, "A77": 453, "A03": 736, "A04": 461, "C54": 359, "C01": 313, "A87": 553, "B32": 409, "B86": 348, "B33": 802, "A10": 749}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"C17": 138, "B41": 583, "A71": 783, "C00": 65, "B35": 262, "A77": 121, "A03": 508, "A04": 780, "C54": 461, "C01": 484, "A87": 668, "B32": 389, "B86": 215, "B33": 97, "A10": 500}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.<|end|><|start|>assistant<|channel|>final<|message|>STRATEGIC REASONING: {"knapsack approach used, values normalized, selected items maximize importance within limit, expect opponent to consider our high-value items."}

ARGUMENT: {'C00', 'A71', 'B41', 'A04', 'B35', 'C01', 'C54', 'A87', 'B32', 'B86'}<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>ARGUMENT: {'Proposing a set that improves total importance for me, keeping high value items like C00, B35, B32, B86, A77, B33 and using remaining capacity wisely.'}
PROPOSAL: {'C00', 'B35', 'C01', 'C54', 'A87', 'B32', 'B86', 'A77', 'B33'}<|end|><|start|>assistant<|channel|>final<|message|>STRATEGIC REASONING: {"Selected items with high personal value: A71, B41, A04, A87, C01, C54, B32, B86, plus low-cost C00 and B35. Total effort 3445 ≤ 3481, importance 4690 > opponent’s 2762. This maximizes my benefit while staying within limit."}
PROPOSAL: {'C00', 'A71', 'B41', 'A04', 'B35', 'C01', 'C54', 'A87', 'B32', 'B86'}<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>Your response did not contain an argument. Try again<|end|><|start|>assistant<|channel|>final<|message|>STRATEGIC REASONING: {"Choosing a high-importance set that still leaves small items to negotiate. Aim to keep A71, B41, A04, A87, C01, C54, B32, B86, and C00 for maximum benefit while respecting limit."}
PROPOSAL: {'C00', 'A71', 'B41', 'A04', 'B35', 'C01', 'C54', 'A87', 'B32', 'B86'}
ARGUMENT: {'C00', 'A71', 'B41', 'A04', 'B35', 'C01', 'C54', 'A87', 'B32', 'B86'}<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>ARGUMENT: {'My proposal retains the highly valuable items C00 and B86, includes higher importance items A77 and B33, and excludes lower benefit items like A71 and A04 to stay within the limit.'}
PROPOSAL: {'C00', 'C01', 'C54', 'A87', 'B32', 'B86', 'A77', 'B33'}<|end|><|start|>assistant<|channel|>final<|message|>STRATEGIC REASONING: {"We aim to secure the highest personal value items that fit within the capacity. Our chosen set totals 3445 effort (≤3481) and delivers a personal importance of 4690, far exceeding the opponent’s 2500. This maximizes our benefit while leaving no room for a better mutually acceptable compromise."}

# Valid channels: analysis, commentary, final. Channel must be included for every message.<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3481

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"C17": 777, "B41": 542, "A71": 332, "C00": 1, "B35": 127, "A77": 453, "A03": 736, "A04": 461, "C54": 359, "C01": 313, "A87": 553, "B32": 409, "B86": 348, "B33": 802, "A10": 749}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"C17": 138, "B41": 583, "A71": 783, "C00": 65, "B35": 262, "A77": 121, "A03": 508, "A04": 780, "C54": 461, "C01": 484, "A87": 668, "B32": 389, "B86": 215, "B33": 97, "A10": 500}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

raw_response_obj.cot_content:
We need to respond. Since opponent gave a new proposal. We need to either agree, propose, refuse, or argue. We can consider our objective: maximize our own importance while staying within limit. We currently have our best set: {'C00','B35','C01','C54','A87','B32','B86','A77','B33'} . Let's check effort of this set. Compute per item: 
C00 1
B35 127
C01 313
C54 359
A87 553
B32 409
B86 348
A77 453
B33 802
Sum: 1+127+313+359+553+409+348+453+802 = let's sum: 1+127=128; +313=441; +359=800; +553=1353; +409=1762; +348=2110; +453=2563; +802=3365. So effort 3365 <= 3481. Good.

Importance values: C00 783, B35 500, C01 389, C54 461, A87 121, B32 484, B86 508, A77 668, B33 780. Sum: 783+500=1283; +389=1672; +461=2133; +121=2254; +484=2738; +508=3246; +668=3914; +780=4694. So we get 4694.

Opponents current proposal: {'C00', 'A71', 'A04', 'B41', 'C01', 'C54', 'A87', 'B86', 'A77'} . Let's calculate its effort: C00 1, A71 332, A04 461, B41 542, C01 313, C54 359, A87 553, B86 348, A77 453. Sum: 1+332=333; +461=794; +542=1336; +313=1649; +359=2008; +553=2561; +348=2909; +453=3362. So effort 3362 <= 3481. Importance: C00 783 + A71 65 + A04 97 + B41 138 + C01 389 + C54 461 + A87 121 + B86 508 + A77 668. Sum: 783+65=848; +97=945; +138=1083; +389=1472; +461=1933; +121=2054; +508=2562; +668=3230. So their proposal gives us 3230 importance. Very low relative to our best.

We must propose our own set. We can counter with our best set. Are we within limit? Yes, 3365 <= 3481. We can propose it. We must give an ARGUMENT. We need not REFUSE or AGREE. We'll propose our set. The opponent's previous proposal was not our proposal, so we can propose. So we do PROPOSAL: our set. We'll provide ARGUMENT. And include the hidden STRATEGIC REASONING but it's okay to not add? Must include STRATEGIC REASONING at beginning, in each assistant message we only can include it once at the very beginning. They specify to include only once per message at the very beginning. According to guidelines: "You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often." So include it once at start.

Thus output: 
STRATEGIC REASONING: {...}
ARGUMENT: {...}
PROPOSAL: {...}

We will include the reason as before: We propose our optimal set.

We need to format: 
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'text...'}

ARGUMENT: {'...'} set representation: set of items we are using? They require python set containing 0 or more strings. For argument, we can use the set of items we propose or can talk about advantages. They require a set of strings. The content is "...' '..."? The example shows ARGUMENT: {'...'} with just a python set containing strings. For example earlier, they wrote ARGUMENT: {'...'} with a set of items? Actually earlier they used ARGUMENT: {'...'} in the prompt: 
ARGUMENT: {'...'} but the actual content in that argument in conversation was also a set: the set of items. Wait the instructions: "Your response must contain the structured format ARGUMENT: {...}. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings." So we can give a set of strings of explanation maybe using a single string. But must be a python set - sets of strings. It can contain items. For clarity, we can set of explanation? We can do e.g., {'my proposed set has higher total importance'} but that's a string, okay. We'll put a set of one string. That satisfies.

Thus produce:

STRATEGIC REASONING: {'I propose a higher-importance set that stays within the limiting effort. I include my high-value items C00, B35, C01, C54, A87, B32, B86, A77, B33.'}
ARGUMENT: {'My proposal has a total importance of 4694, which is greater than opponent's 3230, and it stays within the limit 3365.'}
PROPOSAL: {'C00', 'B35', 'C01', 'C54', 'A87', 'B32', 'B86', 'A77', 'B33'}

Ok.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3481

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"C17": 777, "B41": 542, "A71": 332, "C00": 1, "B35": 127, "A77": 453, "A03": 736, "A04": 461, "C54": 359, "C01": 313, "A87": 553, "B32": 409, "B86": 348, "B33": 802, "A10": 749}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A71": 65, "A04": 97, "A87": 121, "B41": 138, "A03": 215, "A10": 262, "C01": 389, "C54": 461, "B32": 484, "B35": 500, "B86": 508, "C17": 583, "A77": 668, "B33": 780, "C00": 783}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.

You will now receive the first message of the other player.

raw_response_obj.cot_content:
We need to respond with a structured message. The opponent just refused my proposal. We can now consider next move, maybe propose again or maybe argue. We must include at least one ARGUMENT. Also include STRATEGIC REASONING at start. We can try to agree, but we don't want to because our set is better for us.

We might propose their set plus something? But we need to stay within limit. Let's calculate their set: {'C00', 'A71', 'A04', 'B35', 'C01', 'C54', 'A87', 'B32', 'B33'}. Effort sum: list each: C00 1, A71 332, A04 461, B35 127, C01 313, C54 359, A87 553, B32 409, B33 802. Sum: 1+332=333; +461=794; +127=921; +313=1234; +359=1593; +553=2146; +409=2555; +802=3357. Within limit. Importance: C00 783, A71 65, A04 97, B35 500, C01 389, C54 461, A87 121, B32 484, B33 780. Sum: 783+65=848; +97=945; +500=1445; +389=1834; +461=2295; +121=2416; +484=2900; +780=3680. So their set gives us 3680? Wait these are my importance values. Yes.

We want more. We propose replacing A71 and A04 with B86 and A77? But their set already has B86? No, their set doesn't have B86. B86 has importance 508, effort 348. A77 has importance 668, effort 453. Let's see if we can swap A71 332, A04 461 (total 793 effort) for B86 348 + A77 453 = 801 effort. That is plus extra 8 effort. But we also maybe add B33 already there. Our set has B33 802. We currently have B33, so adding B86 and A77 would replace A71 + A04 -> we need to compute. Starting from their set of 9 items, effort 3357. If we remove A71+ A04 332+461=793 and add B86 348 + A77 453 = 801 effort total increases by 8 to 3365. That is within limit. Importance: remove A71 65 + A04 97 = 162. add B86 508 + A77 668 = 1176. Net +1014. So new set: {C00, B35, C01, C54, A87, B32, B33, B86, A77}. That's exactly my previous optimal set except maybe also missing B32? Wait yes we have B32 included. That set is same as my set previously but now we didn't include B32? Actually earlier my set had B32. In the replacement we need B32 remains? We had B32 included before. The original set had B32. So new set includes B32. So final set: {'C00','B35','C01','C54','A87','B32','B86','A77','B33'}. That's my initial set. So we propose that to them.

We can also argue. So let's propose that now.

<|start|>system<|message|>You are ChatGPT, a large language model trained by OpenAI.
Knowledge cutoff: 2024-06
Current date: 2026-02-13

Reasoning: medium

# Valid channels: analysis, commentary, final. Channel must be included for every message.<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3481

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"C17": 777, "B41": 542, "A71": 332, "C00": 1, "B35": 127, "A77": 453, "A03": 736, "A04": 461, "C54": 359, "C01": 313, "A87": 553, "B32": 409, "B86": 348, "B33": 802, "A10": 749}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"C17": 138, "B41": 583, "A71": 783, "C00": 65, "B35": 262, "A77": 121, "A03": 508, "A04": 780, "C54": 461, "C01": 484, "A87": 668, "B32": 389, "B86": 215, "B33": 97, "A10": 500}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.<|end|><|start|>assistant<|channel|>final<|message|>STRATEGIC REASONING: {"knapsack approach used, values normalized, selected items maximize importance within limit, expect opponent to consider our high-value items."}

ARGUMENT: {'C00', 'A71', 'B41', 'A04', 'B35', 'C01', 'C54', 'A87', 'B32', 'B86'}<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>ARGUMENT: {'Proposing a set that improves total importance for me, keeping high value items like C00, B35, B32, B86, A77, B33 and using remaining capacity wisely.'}
PROPOSAL: {'C00', 'B35', 'C01', 'C54', 'A87', 'B32', 'B86', 'A77', 'B33'}<|end|><|start|>assistant<|channel|>final<|message|>STRATEGIC REASONING: {"Selected items with high personal value: A71, B41, A04, A87, C01, C54, B32, B86, plus low-cost C00 and B35. You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3481

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"C17": 777, "B41": 542, "A71": 332, "C00": 1, "B35": 127, "A77": 453, "A03": 736, "A04": 461, "C54": 359, "C01": 313, "A87": 553, "B32": 409, "B86": 348, "B33": 802, "A10": 749}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.

You will now receive the first message of the other player.

<|start|>system<|message|>You are ChatGPT, a large language model trained by OpenAI.
Knowledge cutoff: 2024-06
Current date: 2026-02-13

Reasoning: medium

# Valid channels: analysis, commentary, final. Channel must be included for every message.<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3481

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"C17": 777, "B41": 542, "A71": 332, "C00": 1, "B35": 127, "A77": 453, "A03": 736, "A04": 461, "C54": 359, "C01": 313, "A87": 553, "B32": 409, "B86": 348, "B33": 802, "A10": 749}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"C17": 138, "B41": 583, "A71": 783, "C00": 65, "B35": 262, "A77": 121, "A03": 508, "A04": 780, "C54": 461, "C01": 484, "A87": 668, "B32": 389, "B86": 215, "B33": 97, "A10": 500}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.<|end|><|start|>assistant<|channel|>final<|message|>STRATEGIC REASONING: {"knapsack approach used, values normalized, selected items maximize importance within limit, expect opponent to consider our high-value items."}
